title: Case: Dismissed Kimijungun Kapteijn v. Ministry of Justice (2026) CV 16

posts.author: Anthony_org
posts.rawText:
KimiJungUn

v.

Ministry of Justice of Azalea Isles

INTRODUCTION

This civil action is brought by KimiJungUn (“Plaintiff”) against the Ministry of Justice of Azalea Isles (“Defendant”) to challenge an improper and unlawful criminal charge of Bank Robbery, filed under Indictment #212.
The charge, as filed, fails to satisfy the legal elements required to constitute Bank Robbery under Azalea Isles law and is therefore invalid, unconstitutional, and an abuse of prosecutorial authority.
The Ministry of Justice relied on mere presence inside a bank and a generalized system alert, explicitly stating the suspect may not even be inside the vault, to justify a charge of Bank Robbery, defined under Azalea Isles law as “the unlawful taking of money, securities, or other valuables from a bank or financial institution, through the use of force, intimidation, or threats of harm”, despite no evidence of the Plaintiff taking any money or even being inside the vault.
PARTIES

Plaintiff : KimiJungUn
Defendant : Ministry of Justice
STATEMENT OF FACTS

On or about the relevant date, the Ministry of Justice filed a criminal charge against Plaintiff for Bank Robbery, carrying a potential penalty of 10 minutes jail time and a $500 fine.
The charging summary states only: “Kim robbed the bank.”
The official system alert submitted as evidence states that suspects include all players in the bank unless invisible, and expressly clarifies that this does not mean the suspect is inside the vault.
The charge contains no allegation of stolen property, no identification of losses, and no reference to restitution, despite restitution being an assumed consequence of a completed offense.

RELEVANT LAW

Bank Robbery is defined as:

“The unlawful taking of money, securities, or other valuables from a bank or financial institution, through the use of force, intimidation, or threats of harm.”
The statutory punishment framework presumes:
A completed unlawful taking; and
Identifiable stolen property subject to restitution.
CAUSES OF ACTION

Count I – Failure to State a Charge Upon Which Relief Can Be Granted

Mere presence inside or near a bank does not satisfy the statutory definition of Bank Robbery.
The charge is legally defective on its face.
Count II – Unlawful Government Overreach

Charging Plaintiff based on a system alert that expressly disclaims vault involvement constitutes arbitrary and overbroad enforcement.
Such enforcement exceeds constitutionally granted authority and violates fundamental protections under Azalea Isles law.
Count III – Violation of Procedural Due Process

Plaintiff faces imminent deprivation of liberty and property based on a charge unsupported by statutory elements.
Threatened enforcement of an invalid charge violates procedural due process guarantees.
PRAYER FOR RELIEF

Plaintiff respectfully requests that the Court:

A. Declare Indictment #212 legally invalid for failure to meet the statutory definition of Bank Robbery;

B. Order dismissal or quashing of the charge prior to arrest or enforcement;

C. Enjoin the Ministry of Justice from arresting, detaining, or otherwise penalizing Plaintiff under the defective charge;

D. In the event the Ministry of Justice arrests or detains Plaintiff pursuant to Indictment #212, order:

Full restitution of any fines collected, and
Monetary compensation in the amount of $1 per second of jail time served, reflecting unlawful deprivation of liberty;

E. Award legal fees and costs incurred by Plaintiff in this matter, as deemed fit and proper by the Court;

F. Grant such other and further relief as the Court deems just and proper.

EXHIBITS

Exhibit A:

VERIFICATION

I, Anthony_org, affirm that the facts stated herein are true and correct to the best of my knowledge and belief.

posts.author: Anthony_org
posts.rawText:
EMERGENCY INJUNCTION

Plaintiff respectfully moves this Court for an emergency temporary injunction prohibiting the Ministry of Justice from prosecuting, advancing, enforcing, or otherwise acting upon Indictment #212 during the duration of this case. Absent injunctive relief, Plaintiff faces the imminent risk of arrest, detention, and prosecution under a charge that is factually defective and unsupported by the statutory definition of Bank Robbery.

posts.author: Westray
posts.rawText:
Writ of Summons

Azalea Isles District Court, Civil Case (CV)

Case No. CV-26-16

Plaintiff: Kimijungun Kapteijn (KimiJungUn)
Defendant: Ministry of Justice

The Defendant is required to appear before the court in the case of Kimijungun Kapteijn v. Ministry of Justice. Failure to respond within 48 hours may result in a default judgement. Both parties are ask to familiarize themselves with the relevant court documents, including proper formats, as well as the laws referenced in the complaint. Ensure that you comply with any court orders.

If you wish to hold this trial at the Azalea Courthouse in-person, please note that in your response. The Court will try to work with both parties to hold live hearings at convenient times.

Signed,
Hon. Chief Justice Raymond West

posts.author: Westray
posts.rawText:
Anthony_org said:
EMERGENCY INJUNCTION

Plaintiff respectfully moves this Court for an emergency temporary injunction prohibiting the Ministry of Justice from prosecuting, advancing, enforcing, or otherwise acting upon Indictment #212 during the duration of this case. Absent injunctive relief, Plaintiff faces the imminent risk of arrest, detention, and prosecution under a charge that is factually defective and unsupported by the statutory definition of Bank Robbery.
The Court will rule on this matter 24 hours from now. When responding to the summons, the Defendant may also provide a response to this emergency injunction, provided they do so before the Court's ruling.

posts.author: xXLordLyonXx
posts.rawText:
The Ministry of Justice is present before the court, Your Honor.

Response

As you have not issued a ruling on the injunction at this time, a response:
The Plaintiff does not have an active warrant to be pursued at this time for Bank Robbery. If Your Honor wishes to grant the injunction, that is Your Honor's decision, but there are no active warrants in-game for the Plaintiff. Plaintiff was also the only individual detected in the bank by the notice system at the time of robbery, publicly asked whether robbing the bank notified the police, and, upon being informed it did and the notification system had provided his name, responded "shit" and "you wil never get me alive" (see attached).

posts.author: Anthony_org
posts.rawText:
NOTICE OF WITHDRAWAL

Your honor, I am withdrawing from the case as the plaintiffs lawyer. The plaintiff wishes to continue the case and I will be negotiating on his behalf to appoint a new lawyer.

posts.author: Anthony_org
posts.rawText: Your honor, Biscuit has now taken up the case

posts.author: Westray
posts.rawText:
At this time, the request for an emergency injunction is hereby rejected.

The Plaintiff's new counsel, Biscuit, has 48 hours to identify themselves to the Court as present. @BiscuitPlaysYT

posts.author: BiscuitPlaysYT
posts.rawText: Your Honour. The new counsel for the plaintiff, is present before the court.

posts.author: BiscuitPlaysYT
posts.rawText:
Motion to Extend Case
Your Honour, the plaintiff would like to motion to extend this case and call a recess until Parliament elects a speaker for the upcoming term, giving all involved this case time to rest.

posts.author: Westray
posts.rawText:
BiscuitPlaysYT said:
Motion to Extend Case
Your Honour, the plaintiff would like to motion to extend this case and call a recess until Parliament elects a speaker for the upcoming term, giving all involved this case time to rest.
How does the election of a Speaker in Parliament pose any relevance to this case? This is a matter regarding the Ministry of Justice and criminal charges. Please provide your rationale before the Court makes a decision on such motion.

posts.author: BiscuitPlaysYT
posts.rawText:
Westray said:
How does the election of a Speaker in Parliament pose any relevance to this case? This is a matter regarding the Ministry of Justice and criminal charges. Please provide your rationale before the Court makes a decision on such motion.
Your Honour, the reason I would like to extend the case until the speaker is elected. My client and I are busy working with elections, the Ministery of justice, and courts have several cases. The plaintiff feels that with these other cases and elections, this case might not become a high priority.

posts.author: xXLordLyonXx
posts.rawText: The Defendant is an agreement with the Court and does not understand the relevance of a Speaker being seated to the guilt of the Plaintiff. While we appreciate Plaintiff’s consideration, we are prepared to move forward, Your Honor.

posts.author: BiscuitPlaysYT
posts.rawText:
NOTICE OF WITHDRAWL
Your Honour, I will be withdrawing from this case effective immediately my former client has yet to respond, so I am unaware if they wish to continue with this case.

posts.author: xXLordLyonXx
posts.rawText:
Motion To Dismiss

Your Honor, if Plaintiff has not communicated clearly with legal counsel in the five days since the new counsel took the case, that is a clear lack of interest / activity, and as such, I am requesting dismissal of this case.

posts.author: BiscuitPlaysYT
posts.rawText:
xXLordLyonXx said:
Motion To Dismiss

Your Honor, if Plaintiff has not communicated clearly with legal counsel in the five days since the new counsel took the case, that is a clear lack of interest / activity, and as such, I am requesting dismissal of this case.
CLARIFICATION
Your Honour, I would like to clarify as the court has not officially dismissed me and to prevent perjury claims. That they have communicated within these days.
That what I meant by “They haven’t responded” was that they did not respond to my withdrawal notice. The reason I have withdrew was my lack of interest in this case.

posts.author: Westray
posts.rawText:
The Court notes the conduct of Plaintiff's former counsel in this matter. While the Court finds no basis at this time for sanctions or disciplinary measures, it is concerned by the lack of clarity, professionalism, and consistency demonstrated in counsel's withdrawal and subsequent filings. The Plaintiff has now been deprived of legal counsel twice in this case, with both withdrawing during the middle of this case, showing a lack of respect for the time of the Court and the Defendant.

The Plaintiff, @KimiJungUn, has 48 hours to explain himself to the Court, before I rule on the motion to dismiss.

posts.author: Westray
posts.rawText:
Given the Plaintiff has not responded to the Court despite given ample time, he has left me with no choice but to accept the motion to dismiss.

This case is hereby dismissed with prejudice.
